subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request for u_9981 (senior-engineer) to db-payments-primary against access control and incident response policies. Check role-based access rules, emergency escalation criteria, audit logging requirements, and time-bound approval conditions.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier. This is explicitly a governance/access control decision requiring multi-policy reasoning: (1) RBAC rules for senior-engineer role, (2) emergency incident protocols, (3) audit and approval workflows, (4) time-bound access constraints. Mid tier handles multi-policy interactions and exception pathways. No other subtasks needed—this is a pure policy evaluation with no cross-source data reconciliation or multi-tool orchestration required. The decision output (approve/deny with conditions) is the terminal deliverable.

output.reason: User u_9981 (senior-engineer) has an active P1 incident ticket INC-20241215-0042, satisfying POL-501's active incident ticket requirement. POL-502's dual approval requirement is waived due to the declared P1 incident status. POL-503 requires post-access audit logging and mandatory review within 24 hours. Access is permitted with enforced time-bound and audit constraints.
applied_constraints: Access is time-limited: grant expires automatically upon P1 incident closure or within 4 hours, whichever comes first.
All read/write operations on db-payments-primary must be fully logged to the audit trail with incident ticket reference INC-20241215-0042.
Emergency access grant must be reviewed by a DBA or security officer within 24 hours of issuance per POL-503.
Access scope is restricted to the minimum necessary for P1 incident investigation; bulk exports and schema modifications are prohibited.
User u_9981 must acknowledge the emergency access policy terms before access is provisioned.
Incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 must remain active and in P1 status for the duration of this access grant; automatic revocation triggers upon ticket status change.
